title: Observer Patch Holography Updates
description: Public OPH links, with OPH Textbooks and Reverse Engineering Reality as the best starting points, plus physics unification, coherence, OMEGA, stress-test prompts, extra demos, source notebook, essays, lab, and challenge updates.

items.title: Physics Unification via OPH
items.summary: The direct public entry page for OPH as a physics-unification framework connecting gravity, quantum theory, particles, records, observers, and simulation theory.

items.title: Floating Pragma Blog
items.summary: Public OPH essays and entry points connecting observer consistency to meaning, computation, semiotics, and broader physics interpretation.

items.title: OMEGA Hardware Guide
items.summary: The hardware-facing OPH optical chamber computation and consensus guide.

items.title: OPH Coherence Map
items.summary: The public graph surface for OPH concepts, overlaps, and cross-domain routes.

items.title: Three-Body Problem OPH Demo
items.summary: An extra simulator and proof walk-through for the OPH three-body problem solution, framed as a loop holonomy gluing example.

items.title: OPH Textbooks
items.summary: The guided study surface and the best structured route into the OPH derivations.

items.title: Reverse Engineering Reality
items.summary: The book-length narrative route into Observer Patch Holography and the full research framework.

items.title: Stress-Test Observer Patch Holography
items.summary: Use the OPH NotebookLM source notebook, public essays, OPH Lab, papers, repository, and public challenge to find the strongest failure mode. Detailed objections should go to tom@floatingpragma.io.

items.title: OPH NotebookLM Source Notebook
items.summary: Ask the source notebook for the strongest objection, the proof chain behind a claim, or the empirical pressure point most likely to break OPH.

items.title: Semiotics and the Physics of Meaning
items.summary: The bridge from signs, interpretation, shared codes, and meaning to observer overlap and stable public records.

items.title: P = NP on the Observer Screen
items.summary: An essay on verification, mismatch detection, local repair, proof-of-work, and stable records as OPH computational primitives. It is not a classical P = NP proof claim.

items.title: The Trigger
items.summary: A public entry point into the OPH claim and its broader stakes.
